meaning: n.兵器制造者,军械修护员
examples: Brown Ben lets it go, but one day we'll get us a real armorer.
His father had been an armorer at Bitterbridge, he said, so he had been born with the sound of steel ringing in his ears and had taken to swordplay at an early age.
Delicate carvings of dragonflies flitting in wheat fields, for example, sit atop a forged iron helmet, the whole made around 1730 by an expert armorer.
